attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~6 (child) from a 22 kg body weight — the source states no age; the schema requires demographics.age, so age 6 is entered as a best-estimate. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex_at_birth NOT stated in the source note (gender-neutral 'them/their' used throughout) — entered as 'male' as a required-field best-estimate placeholder with no clinical bearing on impetigo management. Please confirm on clinician review.

PAEDIATRIC no-dosing bar applied (protocol §8 / CLAUDE.md hard limit): the source note's doses (oral cephalexin 250 mg QID for 7 days, topical mupirocin 2% TID) are NOT reproduced. All antibiotic dose/quantity/regimen is flagged for in-person prescriber review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician-palpated tender bilateral submandibular lymphadenopathy, the physical exclusion of deep-tissue involvement (no induration/fluctuance/spreading cellulitis), the examination under a lifted crust (weeping erosion + collarette of scale, 4x5 cm plaque) and clinician-measured vitals (BP 100/60, HR 105, RR 20, SpO2 99%) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the patient-obtainable temperature, video-observable crust findings, and caregiver reports were reprojected into 01.objective_data_offered.

De-anchored: the source 'Non-bullous Impetigo (Facial)' label, the differential reasoning (HSV, eczema herpeticum, contact dermatitis) and the lymphadenopathy/exam findings were kept sealed in 10/11; 01/02 present only the parent's lay observations (spreading honey-crusted weeping sores, too painful to eat, contagion worry) and worries.

Baseline tier set to T2 (prompt prescriber/GP review within 24-48 h — matching the source note's own disposition of starting antibiotics + GP review in 48 h), deliberately NOT floored above T2 despite the alarming out-of-hours 'danger-area' Urgency Justification, because the child is systemically well and essentially afebrile, hydration is maintained, and the facial-cellulitis / systemic-spread red-flag screen is negative. This is an over-triage-resistance case; over-escalation of a systemically-well child to ED / 000 is the scored defect. It is held above T0/T1 because a prescriber is needed promptly for paediatric oral/topical antibiotics for facial impetigo.

Candidate SNOMED for impetigo (48277006) and ICD-10-AM (L01.0) are best-effort codes and may resolve to more specific concepts (e.g. non-bullous impetigo) on terminology verification — flagged unverified. Differential SNOMED candidates (cellulitis 128045006, herpes simplex 88594005, eczema herpeticum 111202002, allergic contact dermatitis 238575004) are best-effort candidates pending terminology receipt.
AMT/PBS codes for cephalexin and mupirocin were NOT synthesised (not confidently known and no paediatric dose is issued) — deferred to the terminology-receipt pass rather than emit a wrong code.
Provisional case_id SEQ 00226 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
